metadata.seed: Yeah. I was at a party, and I was kind of hesitant about going, and then I went and I realized, like, I just can't talk. Like, I don't like meeting new people. I never know what to say. And then it's, like, an awkward silence, and then I just feel like I don't want to be there anymore.

patient.query: Yeah. I was at a party, and I was kind of hesitant about going, and then I went and I realized, like, I just can't talk. Like, I don't like meeting new people. I never know what to say. And then it's, like, an awkward silence, and then I just feel like I don't want to be there anymore.

automatic_thoughts: I never know what to say

entailment: Fear of being in a small group (finding)

patient.query: Yeah, I get that. But sometimes it just feels like everyone is judging me, even if they’re not. I wish I could just let go and be myself without all that pressure. It’s like I’m stuck in my own head, and I don’t know how to break out of it.

patient.query: I mean, I try, but it’s just hard to see it that way. In my mind, I always imagine the worst—like everyone is looking at me and thinking something negative. It’s like this little voice that just won't shut up. Honestly, I don't know if I could trust myself to think differently in those moments. It just feels... safer to assume the worst.

automatic_thoughts: everyone is looking at me and thinking something negative
I can't trust myself to think differently
it's safer to assume the worst

neutral: Ability to resist dangerous thoughts (observable entity)
Anxiety about saying the wrong thing (finding)

patient.query: I guess I don’t really have solid evidence. It’s more of a... feeling, you know? Like, I’ll see someone’s expression change and my mind jumps to thinking they’re judging me. I wish I could just accept that maybe they’re not, but instead, I just spiral. It’s frustrating because deep down, I know it's probably all in my head, but in the moment, it feels so real.

automatic_thoughts: it's probably all in my head

patient.query: I don’t know... maybe? It just feels so automatic. When I look at someone and they seem indifferent or bored, my mind immediately jumps to "they don’t want me here" or "I’m embarrassing myself.” I think I just get so caught up in my own insecurities that I can’t see it any other way. It’s like a loop that I can’t escape.

Sometimes, even just a quiet moment makes me think I’m doing something wrong, even if it’s not true.
